issues.description: FR-2b states 'Environment variables that existed pre-migration (NAUTILOOP_API_KEY) continue to work and override the active profile's value.' However, NAUTILOOP_API_KEY is NOT read by the CLI — it is only used server-side (control-plane env, terraform, k8s manifests). The CLI loads api_key exclusively from ~/.nemo/config.toml. This requirement is based on a false premise.
issues.suggestion: Either (a) remove FR-2b entirely, since there is no CLI env-var behavior to preserve, or (b) rewrite FR-2b as a NEW feature: 'Add NAUTILOOP_API_KEY env-var support that overrides the active profile's api_key.' If it's a new feature, add an acceptance criterion for it.

issues.description: FR-3a requires 'nemo profile add' to be interactive (prompt for server_url, api_key, engineer) and FR-3b says missing flags prompt. NFR-2 requires 'No new dependencies.' The CLI has no interactive prompting library (no dialoguer, inquire, or stdin readline infrastructure). Implementing interactive prompts without a new dependency means raw stdin reads, which is fragile and inconsistent with the rest of the CLI.
issues.suggestion: Either (a) relax NFR-2 to allow a prompting crate like dialoguer/inquire, (b) make all fields required flags with no interactive fallback (non-interactive only), or (c) explicitly specify that raw stdin prompting is acceptable and describe the expected UX (e.g., 'server_url [http://localhost:18080]: ').

issues.description: The current EngineerConfig struct has `models: ModelsSection` and `helm: HelmConfig` as direct fields alongside server_url/engineer/etc. The spec says these move to root-level after migration while connection fields move into [profiles.*]. This requires splitting one struct into two (ProfileConfig for per-profile fields, RootConfig for global + profiles map). The spec doesn't address how the internal config API changes — e.g., code that currently does `config.helm.desktop_notifications` or `config.models.implementor` needs a new access pattern.
issues.suggestion: Add a brief implementation note clarifying the expected struct layout: e.g., a top-level NemoConfig with `current_profile`, `profiles: HashMap<String, ProfileConfig>`, `helm: HelmConfig`, `models: ModelsSection`, and a resolved accessor method that returns the active profile's fields merged with root settings.

issues.description: FR-6c says 'Profile-specific vs root-global disambiguation is by the dotted path; root-level sections ([helm], [models]) remain at root.' But the disambiguation rule is implicit. What happens with `nemo config --set engineer=foo` — does that write to the active profile? What about `nemo config --set models.implementor=gpt-4`? The spec needs an explicit rule for which top-level keys are profile-scoped vs root-scoped.
issues.suggestion: Add an explicit allow-list: 'Profile-scoped keys: server_url, api_key, engineer, name, email. Root-scoped keys: anything under helm.* or models.*. Unrecognized keys are rejected with an error.' This removes ambiguity.

issues.description: FR-3c requires tab completion for profile names via clap's completion generation. The CLI does not currently use clap_complete (not in dependencies). Dynamic completions for profile names require reading the config file at completion time, which is more complex than static clap completions.
issues.suggestion: Either (a) add clap_complete as an allowed new dependency (contradicts NFR-2 as written, but it's a clap ecosystem crate), (b) defer tab completion to a follow-up, or (c) clarify that shell completion scripts are generated statically and profile name completion is best-effort.

issues.description: FR-4b introduces NAUTILOOP_PROFILE env var but there is no acceptance criterion for it beyond AC #4 which tests it. There's no specification of what happens when NAUTILOOP_PROFILE is set to an empty string, or when both NAUTILOOP_PROFILE and --profile are set to different non-existent profiles (which error message wins?).
issues.suggestion: Add: 'Empty NAUTILOOP_PROFILE is treated as unset. When --profile is provided, NAUTILOOP_PROFILE is ignored entirely (including for error messages).' The existing precedence chain covers the happy path but edge cases need a sentence.

issues.description: FR-3a says 'nemo profile add' defaults come 'from current if applicable' but doesn't specify which fields. server_url defaulting from the current profile seems wrong (you're adding a NEW cluster). engineer/name/email defaulting from current makes sense since it's the same person.
issues.suggestion: Specify: 'Defaults for name and email are copied from the current profile. server_url and api_key have no default and must be provided.'

issues.description: Problem Statement says 'api-key flag doesn't exist currently; users set NAUTILOOP_API_KEY env var instead.' This is doubly incorrect: there is no --api-key flag AND the CLI doesn't read NAUTILOOP_API_KEY either. The env var is server-side only.
issues.suggestion: Correct to: 'There is no --api-key flag and the CLI does not read API keys from environment variables; the only source is the config file.'
